headline: RM31.3B Recovered, RM24.46B Still Owed, RM6B Still In Arbitration
context: MACC said 1MDB recovery hit RM31.3B in May 2026 — 74.5% of RM42B. MOF replies show RM5.29B remains in the trust account, RM24.46B is still owed, and Goldman is contesting USD1.4B in London arbitration.

cards.big: Malaysia's RM31.3B 1MDB recovery headline includes a Goldman guarantee Goldman is fighting to reduce
cards.sub: Of Goldman's USD3.9B 2020 settlement, USD2.5B was cash. The remaining USD1.4B (about RM6B) is an asset-recovery guarantee Goldman filed at the LCIA in October 2023 to contest.

cards.big: Of Goldman's USD3.9B 2020 settlement, only USD2.5B was paid in cash
cards.sub: Goldman filed at the LCIA on 11 October 2023, arguing Malaysia had already recovered enough to satisfy the USD1.4B guarantee. MOF told Parliament in October 2025 the dispute is still in arbitration.

cards.big: Of cumulative 1MDB recoveries, RM5.29B remains in the trust account against RM24.46B still owed
cards.sub: MOF told Parliament in November 2025 that of RM31.2B recovered into the trust account, only RM5.29B remained — the rest paid down 1MDB debt. RM24.46B in obligations is still outstanding.

cards.big: MACC's '74.5% recovered' uses 1MDB's 2015 debt as the denominator. The DOJ alleges US$4.5B was misappropriated.
cards.sub: RM42B is 1MDB's total 2015 debt. DOJ's June 2024 release alleged 'more than US$4.5 billion' (~RM21B) was diverted 2009-2015. The numerator mixes principal, bank settlements, and penalties.

cards.big: The question is not how much was recovered. It is which number the next press release counts.
Think of the headline like a sales total that includes a customer disputing the charge
cards.sub: Malaysia put the full RM31.3B on the receipt. RM6B of it is a Goldman guarantee still in arbitration. Most of what cleared the trust has already gone to service old 1MDB debt.

cards.big: RM31.3B recovered is real. '74.5% of a settled scandal' is not yet what the ledger supports.
cards.sub: It counts a Goldman tranche in arbitration, uses 2015 debt as denominator, mixes principal with penalties, and obscures that RM24.46B is still owed. Holding these figures straight is the work.
